creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text: The TRIMs work, so we shouldn't proactively disable them. FreeBSD is about tools, not policy. Disabling trim administratively is easy enough. Your workload isn't everybody's work load. There may be some work loads where trims are the right thing, or where you want to TRIM the drive when you newfs or fsck but not operationally. We have knobs to do all those things.
